documents.text: The [BRAND] [MODEL] random orbital sander is built for smooth finishing on wood, filler, and painted surfaces. A 280-watt motor delivers 7,000 to 13,000 orbits per minute, helping reduce swirl marks while maintaining steady material removal. Its 125 mm hook-and-loop pad supports quick disc changes, and the sealed dust canister keeps the workspace cleaner during extended jobs. A soft-grip top and compact body improve control on tabletops, doors, and shelving. This model also includes variable speed control, a low-vibration design, and an integrated port for connection to a workshop vacuum.

Designed for corners and narrow edges, the [BRAND] [MODEL] detail sander offers precise control in restoration and decorating projects. It uses a 180-watt motor and oscillates at up to 14,000 cycles per minute for efficient paint removal and surface prep. The triangular sanding pad reaches into tight spots around window frames, stair rails, and furniture profiles. A micro-filtration dust box captures fine particles, while the slim rubberized handle supports one-handed use. The package includes assorted sanding sheets, a finger attachment for confined areas, and a quick-clamp system for fast paper replacement.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] belt sander is suited to fast stock removal on hardwood, softwood, and heavy sanding tasks. Powered by a 900-watt motor, it drives a 75 x 533 mm belt at speeds up to 380 meters per minute, making it effective for leveling uneven boards and stripping finishes. An automatic belt tracking system helps maintain alignment, and the front auxiliary handle improves stability under load. The unit features a lock-on switch for continuous operation, a dust collection bag, and a flat-top design that allows the sander to be inverted for bench-mounted applications.

For everyday smoothing and finishing, the [BRAND] [MODEL] quarter-sheet sander provides reliable performance in a lightweight format. Its 220-watt motor produces 12,000 orbits per minute, suitable for sanding doors, trims, cabinets, and patch repairs. The rectangular base accepts standard quarter sheets, reducing consumable costs and making replacements simple. A low-profile body improves control near edges, while the vibration-dampened handle adds comfort during longer sessions. This sander includes a punch plate for creating dust holes, a rear dust bag, and a durable clamp system that keeps paper securely in place during use.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] drywall sander is engineered for sanding plaster, joint compound, and large wall areas with less strain. A 750-watt motor powers the 225 mm disc head, with variable speed from 800 to 1,800 rpm for controlled finishing. Its extendable handle reaches ceilings and tall partitions, while the pivoting head follows surface contours for more even contact. Built-in LED work lights help reveal scratches and high spots in low-light conditions. This model supports direct vacuum connection, includes several sanding discs, and folds down for easier transport and storage between job sites.

Compact but capable, the [BRAND] [MODEL] palm sander is a practical choice for light finishing, fine sanding, and touch-up work. It runs on a 160-watt motor and offers a fixed speed of 13,500 orbits per minute, ideal for preparing wood before staining or painting. The ergonomic one-hand grip and balanced weight help reduce fatigue on small projects such as drawers, chair legs, and decorative panels. Hook-and-loop pad compatibility allows quick abrasive changes, while the onboard dust collector helps keep surfaces visible. A sealed switch design adds durability when working in dusty workshop conditions.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] cordless random orbital sander combines mobility with consistent finishing performance. Its 18-volt battery platform powers a brushless motor delivering up to 10,000 orbits per minute, making it suitable for site work where outlets are limited. The 125 mm pad handles paint prep, edge blending, and finish sanding on wood and metal. Variable speed settings allow better control across different materials, and the electronic brake stops the pad quickly when the trigger is released. With a detachable dust box, low-vibration housing, and lightweight build, it is designed for convenience and cleaner operation.

Built for demanding renovation work, the [BRAND] [MODEL] variable-speed belt sander balances aggressive sanding with user control. A 1,010-watt motor drives a 76 x 457 mm belt through six speed settings, allowing the tool to adapt from rapid stripping to finer surface leveling. The front roller reaches close to vertical edges, making it useful for flooring, doors, and beams. A wide dust extraction port improves chip removal and can be paired with a vacuum for cleaner sanding. Soft-start electronics, an overmold rear handle, and tool-free belt changes enhance both comfort and productivity.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] finishing sander is tailored for smooth final passes on furniture, trim, and cabinet work. Equipped with a 200-watt motor, it produces 11,000 orbits per minute and uses a vibration-reducing counterbalance system for better handling. Its square sanding base works well on flat panels and along edges, while the paper clamp design accepts precut sheets or roll stock. A clear dust container lets users monitor buildup and empty it quickly. The compact housing fits comfortably in the hand, and the integrated vacuum adapter makes this model easy to connect to shop extraction systems.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] detail mouse sander is intended for hobbyists and home users who need access to tight, awkward spaces. A 130-watt motor provides enough power for sanding shutters, skirting boards, furniture corners, and small craft projects. The pointed pad design improves reach, and the replaceable tip extends pad life when working repeatedly in confined areas. This model includes a dust-sealed on/off switch, a compact collection canister, and a cable strain relief for durability. Weighing just over one kilogram, it remains easy to maneuver for overhead touch-ups and prolonged detailed sanding tasks.

Designed for workshop versatility, the [BRAND] [MODEL] oscillating spindle and belt sander handles curved edges, contours, and straight surfaces in one machine. Its 450-watt induction motor supports both vertical spindle sanding and horizontal belt sanding for shaping shelves, toy parts, and custom joinery components. The table is cast for stability and includes inserts for different drum diameters to improve dust control around the work area. Multiple spindle sleeves and a sanding belt are supplied, allowing fast setup for varied tasks. A built-in storage compartment keeps accessories organized between jobs.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] inline sander is made for bodywork, panel leveling, and sanding broad flat sections with a straight stroke pattern. Powered by a 350-watt motor, it delivers up to 3,000 strokes per minute across a long sanding shoe, helping create uniform finishes on doors, body panels, and composite sheets. Its elongated base reduces the risk of uneven sanding on larger surfaces, while the dual-handle layout improves control during horizontal and vertical use. This unit features external dust extraction compatibility, quick paper clamps, and a reinforced housing intended for repeated professional shop use.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] compact disc sander is a bench-mounted option for precise edge sanding and small part shaping. A 370-watt motor spins the 150 mm sanding disc at 1,450 rpm, offering controlled material removal for wood, plastic, and light nonferrous workpieces. The tilting work table adjusts up to 45 degrees for angled sanding, and the miter guide supports repeatable results on craft and joinery tasks. Rubber feet reduce vibration on the bench, while the guarded design improves user confidence. Dust port connectivity helps keep the machine cleaner during repeated workshop use.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] heavy-duty orbital floor sander is designed for restoring timber floors, decking boards, and large wooden surfaces. Its 1,200-watt motor delivers strong, consistent performance, while the wide sanding base helps cover more area with each pass. A sturdy wheeled chassis and foldable handle simplify transport and setup in residential or commercial spaces. The sander features a high-capacity dust bag, adjustable handle height, and a durable aluminum housing for long service life. Suitable for finish removal and surface preparation, it offers a balanced design that helps reduce operator fatigue over extended sessions.

For automotive refinishing and fine workshop applications, the [BRAND] [MODEL] electric dual-action sander offers controlled cutting and excellent finish quality. The 300-watt motor combines orbital and rotational action to reduce visible sanding patterns on primers, fillers, and painted panels. A 150 mm backing pad supports hook-and-loop abrasives, and the speed dial allows adjustment from 4,000 to 10,000 opm depending on the stage of work. The slim barrel grip improves comfort, while the rear exhaust-style dust outlet connects to extraction equipment. Electronic load sensing helps maintain consistent speed under pressure.

The [BRAND] [MODEL] mini electric file sander is ideal for shaping, deburring, and sanding in narrow spaces where larger tools cannot reach. Its 400-watt motor drives a slim abrasive belt that works effectively on metal edges, weld areas, pipework, and intricate wooden profiles. The adjustable sanding arm can be angled for improved access in awkward positions, and tool-free belt replacement speeds up workflow. A lock-on function supports continuous operation during repetitive tasks, while the side handle enhances stability. Compact dimensions and a durable gear housing make it a practical choice for detailed fabrication work.
